info.title: Cascade
info.description: UX framework for parametric nuclear Monte Carlo simulations. Supports OpenMC and Serpent 2 via a YAML geometry DSL with built-in parametric sweep orchestration.

post.description:
Validate YAML geometry text without expanding or storing it.

Returns immediately — no containers, no files. Suitable for calling
on every editor keystroke (with debounce on the frontend).

Returns a list of structured errors with component and field names
so the editor can underline the offending lines.

Build a 3D scene description from YAML geometry text.

Parses and expands the YAML, then maps each component to
Three.js-renderable objects (cylinder layers, wireframe boxes).
Returns colors, opacity, and bounds — the frontend renders directly.

Called when validation passes and the preview needs to update.

get.description: List all saved geometry definitions, most recently created first.

schema.title: Response List Geometries Api Geometry  Get

post.description:
Save a geometry project's YAML text.

Unlike the old behaviour, this does NOT require the geometry to be
valid or expandable. A geometry project tab in the frontend autosaves
on every edit, and the user is typing invalid/incomplete YAML for
most of that time — rejecting drafts would make autosave useless.

Validity is only enforced where it actually matters: job submission
(POST /jobs/submit) parses and expands the YAML itself and will
reject an invalid geometry there.

The only requirement here is that the YAML parses as a mapping —
i.e. loader.load() doesn't raise a structural LoadError. Pydantic
field-validation errors on individual components (e.g. a FuelPin
with a missing required field) are fine; n_surfaces/n_cells will
just read 0 for those components until they're fixed.

put.description:
Update an existing geometry project's text (autosave target).

Same relaxed-validity rules as save_geometry — drafts save freely.

get.description: Retrieve a saved geometry by ID.

delete.description: Delete a saved geometry by ID.

delete.description:
Delete all materials belonging to a specific library tag.

Useful for removing an entire imported library set at once.
Does not affect materials with different tags.
Builtin materials (library_tag='builtin') cannot be deleted this way.

Get current status and detail for a job.

get.description:
Return the raw stdout from a job's run.log file.

This is the actual OpenMC terminal output — particle transport
progress, k-eff per batch, timing, and errors.
Polled every 2s by the frontend while a job is running.

Returns:
    { "lines": str, "available": bool }

schema.title: Response Get Job Stdout Api Jobs  Job Id  Stdout Get
Response Get Summary Api Results  Job Id  Summary Get
Response Get Tallies Api Results  Job Id  Tallies Get

get.description:
Return the 3-D mesh tally as a structured response.

Response schema::

    {
      "job_id": "...",
      "tally_id": 200,
      "mesh": {
        "type": "regular",
        "shape": [nx, ny, nz],
        "lower_left":  [x0, y0, z0],
        "upper_right": [x1, y1, z1]
      },
      "scores": ["flux", "fission", "heating-local"],
      "data": [
        {"ix": 0, "iy": 0, "iz": 0, "flux_mean": ..., "flux_std_dev": ...},
        ...
      ]
    }

The ``data`` array is ordered (ix, iy, iz) with ix varying slowest —
consistent with NumPy C-order flattening of a (nx, ny, nz) array.
For large meshes (>50³) consider the binary /mesh/raw endpoint instead.

schema.title: Response Get Mesh Api Results  Job Id  Mesh Get
Response Get Spectra Api Results  Job Id  Spectra Get

get.description:
Return the filesystem path of the final statepoint file.

Useful for the frontend to construct a download link or display
the file location to advanced users who want to load it themselves.

Response::

    {"job_id": "...", "path": "/home/user/.cascade/jobs/<id>/output/statepoint.100.h5"}

schema.title: Response Get Statepoint Path Api Results  Job Id  Statepoint Path Get
Response List Profiles Api Jobs Backends Profiles  Get

post.description:
Create a new named backend profile.

Config is validated against the backend type's Pydantic model before saving.
The 'default' name is reserved — edit it via PUT instead.

get.description: Get a single backend profile by name.

put.description:
Replace a profile's config and description.

created_at is preserved. updated_at is bumped.

delete.description:
Delete a profile.

The 'default' profile cannot be deleted — edit it instead.
Returns 204 No Content on success.

nuclear_data_path.title: Nuclear Data Path
nuclear_data_path.description: Host path to the nuclear data directory containing cross_sections.xml. Mounted read-only into every simulation environment.

jobs_base_dir.title: Jobs Base Dir
jobs_base_dir.description: Host path where job working directories are created.

cli.title: Cli
cli.description: Container CLI to use. 'podman' is recommended on Linux.

openmc_bin.title: Openmc Bin
openmc_bin.description: Absolute path to the OpenMC binary inside the container.

nuclear_data_container_path.title: Nuclear Data Container Path
nuclear_data_container_path.description: Container path where nuclear_data_path is mounted.

cpu_limit.title: Cpu Limit
cpu_limit.description: Container CPU limit. '0' = no limit. '2.0' = max 2 CPU cores.

DockerBackendConfig.title: DockerBackendConfig
DockerBackendConfig.description:
Configuration for the Docker/Podman container backend.

Runs OpenMC inside a container on the local machine.
Suitable for development, single-machine research, and testing.

Example frontend payload:
    backend_config:
      type: docker
      image: cascade-openmc:latest
      cli: podman
      memory_limit: 8g
      openmc_bin: /opt/miniconda/envs/openmc/bin/openmc

EnergySpectraRequest.title: EnergySpectraRequest
EnergySpectraRequest.description: Controls flux-vs-energy spectra per material region.

GeometryTextRequest.title: GeometryTextRequest
GeometryTextRequest.description: Raw YAML text from the editor.

nuclear_data_path.title: Nuclear Data Path
nuclear_data_path.description: Host path to the nuclear data directory containing cross_sections.xml. Mounted read-only into every simulation environment.

jobs_base_dir.title: Jobs Base Dir
jobs_base_dir.description: Host path where job working directories are created.

openmc_bin.title: Openmc Bin
openmc_bin.description: Path to the OpenMC binary. Use 'openmc' if it's on PATH, or an absolute path otherwise.

nuclear_data_container_path.title: Nuclear Data Container Path
nuclear_data_container_path.description: Not used for local backend — nuclear_data_path is used directly.

LocalBackendConfig.title: LocalBackendConfig
LocalBackendConfig.description:
Configuration for the local process backend.

Runs OpenMC as a direct subprocess — no container required.
Requires OpenMC to be installed and on PATH (or at openmc_bin path).

Useful when the user has OpenMC installed via conda/pip on their machine
and doesn't want to run a container.

Example frontend payload:
    backend_config:
      type: local
      openmc_bin: /home/user/miniconda/envs/openmc/bin/openmc

name.title: Name
name.description: Unique profile name. Alphanumeric, dashes, underscores.

ProfileResponse.title: ProfileResponse
ProfileResponse.description: Wire format for a backend profile.

ResultsConfigRequest.title: ResultsConfigRequest
ResultsConfigRequest.description: What to ask OpenMC to capture.  All groups default to the cheapest option.

scores.title: Scores
scores.description: TallyScore values to measure per cell.

ScalarTallyRequest.title: ScalarTallyRequest
ScalarTallyRequest.description: Controls per-cell scalar tallies (flux, fission, absorption, heating).

nuclear_data_path.title: Nuclear Data Path
nuclear_data_path.description: Host path to the nuclear data directory containing cross_sections.xml. Mounted read-only into every simulation environment.

jobs_base_dir.title: Jobs Base Dir
jobs_base_dir.description: Host path where job working directories are created.

host.title: Host
host.description: SLURM login node hostname or IP address.

ssh_key_path.title: Ssh Key Path
ssh_key_path.description: Path to the SSH private key on the LOCAL machine.

tasks_per_node.title: Tasks Per Node
tasks_per_node.description: MPI tasks per node (= CPU cores for OpenMC).

walltime.title: Walltime
walltime.description: Maximum wall time in HH:MM:SS format.

account.title: Account
account.description: SLURM account/project to charge. Leave None if not required.

openmc_module.title: Openmc Module
openmc_module.description: Environment module to load before running OpenMC. e.g. 'OpenMC/0.15.3-foss-2023a'. If None, openmc_bin is used directly.

openmc_bin.title: Openmc Bin
openmc_bin.description: OpenMC binary path on the CLUSTER (not local machine). Only used when openmc_module is None.

remote_work_dir.title: Remote Work Dir
remote_work_dir.description: Directory on the CLUSTER where job files are staged. Must be writable and accessible from compute nodes. e.g. '/scratch/username/cascade_jobs'

SlurmBackendConfig.title: SlurmBackendConfig
SlurmBackendConfig.description:
Configuration for the SLURM HPC cluster backend.

Submits jobs via SSH to a SLURM login node using sbatch.
Results are transferred back via SCP after completion.

Most university clusters (including MetaCentrum/MUNI) run SLURM.
OpenMC is typically available as an environment module — no container needed.

Example frontend payload:
    backend_config:
      type: slurm
      host: metacentrum.muni.cz
      username: xradovan
      ssh_key_path: /home/user/.ssh/id_ed25519
      partition: gpu
      nodes: 1
      tasks_per_node: 8
      walltime: "04:00:00"
      openmc_module: OpenMC/0.15.3-foss-2023a
      remote_work_dir: /scratch/xradovan/cascade_jobs
      nuclear_data_path: /shared/openmc_data
